_patch_meta.description: Dimension review patch — C11: 154 group dimension updates (69 confirmed, 28 corrected, 57 newly assigned); 6 Session B findings; 2 Session D pointers

record.finding: Registry 31 contains two distinct sub-domains: (1) moral corruption as inner condition (4899-001, 4900-001, 4901-001, 745-001) and (2) eschatological/thanatological corruption (Hades, Gehenna, Sheol, resurrection hope — assigned Theological/Divine-Human or Spiritual/God-ward). Session B should map how the moral and eschatological uses of corruption relate — do they share a common inner-being logic or are they genuinely distinct?

operations.description: Session B pointer — corruption: moral vs. eschatological sub-domains

record.finding: Registry 57 (evil) is the largest in the programme (46 groups) and functions partly as a container for inner-being framing particles (kol H3605, mi H4310, na H4994, zamam H2161). Session B should assess whether this breadth is analytically productive for the evil analysis or whether the particle-generated groups obscure the core evil vocabulary (poneros, kakia, adikia, etc.).

operations.description: Session B pointer — evil: particle terms produce wide group range

record.finding: Group 851-004 ('the impossibility of inner self-knowledge — the heart's moral corruption exceeds human capacity to understand') is a theologically significant statement about the opacity of the heart to itself. Session B should treat this as a key inner-being anthropological claim and examine its relationship to groups in other registries (e.g. conscience, self-knowledge, moral awareness).

operations.description: Session B pointer — evil: opacity of the heart as anthropological claim

record.finding: Groups 926-001 and 926-002 (ani H6041 — afflicted person) describe the inner experience of the victim of injustice — material need, oppression, and dependence on God for vindication. This is a perspective distinct from the perpetrator-focused groups elsewhere in C11. Session B should examine how the inner experience of being wronged relates to the cluster's primary focus on moral failure as an inner condition.

operations.description: Session B pointer — iniquity: afflicted person as victim perspective in moral-failure cluster

record.finding: The ha.phakh word family (5949 series, 4 groups) encompasses both corrupting perversion and renewing transformation — overturn in either direction. Group 5949-001 assigns Spiritual/God-ward for the mixed group; 5949-002 assigns Affective/Emotional for affective transformation; 5949-003 confirms Theological/Divine-Human for divine inner change. Session B should examine whether perversion and transformation are genuinely polar uses of the same root, and what this reveals about the inner-being concept of transformation in Scripture.

operations.description: Session B pointer — perverseness: ha.phakh as both perversion and transformation

record.finding: The al H0408 particle groups (1119 series, 9 groups) produce a cross-dimensional set spanning fear, anger, grief, rejoicing, trust, pride, concern, and cognitive orientation — the full range of inner-being states negated in divine or human address. Session B should assess whether these groups contribute meaningfully to the analysis of sin as an inner-being phenomenon or whether they represent general inner-being vocabulary included through verse context classification.

operations.description: Session B pointer — sin: al particle groups span full inner-being range

record.description: The Affective/Emotional dimension appears extensively in C11 (15 groups assigned) but was absent from C10 assignments, despite groups in C10 (e.g. innocence 930-007: anguish/dread/hope) that appear to name affective states. The dimension exists in the programme vocabulary and is now confirmed active in C11. Session D should address whether C10 groups should be revisited for Affective/Emotional re-assignment in a subsequent dimension review pass, and whether the automated classifier's keyword triggers for this dimension need adjustment.

The Sin & Vice / Moral/Conscience boundary was systematically refined in C11: Sin & Vice = inner condition of moral failure (wickedness, deceit, corruption as states); Moral/Conscience = evaluative/judicial dimension (verdict, condemnation, moral act). This distinction should be reviewed as a programme-wide vocabulary standard in Session D, as the same boundary question applies to C10 and potentially all clusters.
